name: The Butcher of Dreams — Long Fusion

intro.title: After the closure
intro.narrator: Some days smell tiredness, pepper and things we didn't have time to say.
intro.text:
You're closing the shop.

The neon above the window flashes one last time.

Even today:
- beef ribs,
- sausages,
- customers in a hurry,
- forgotten orders,
- hellos exchanged between two tickets.

Your hands still smell:
pepper,
smoke,
and fresh bread from the nearby bakery.

You're cleaning the big log one last time.

Then you turn the sign:

> CLOSED

Outside, the street is almost empty.

You go home with your old work bag.

Inside:
- a little blanquette pot,
- a well-linked sausage,
- a roasted chicken still warm.

Nothing extraordinary.

Just good things.

Your apartment is quiet.

You put down your keyring.
You take off your shoes.
You light the little lamp by the couch.

Silence does good.

You finally sit down.

For the first time of the day...

You're not doing anything.

The cat jumps on the armrest of the couch.

He's looking at you for a few seconds.

Then he lies against you.

Your eyes are getting heavy.

Very heavy.

And while the city keeps running outside...

hub_start.title: The Song Counter
hub_start.narrator: In dreams, even counters can drift.
hub_start.text:
You open your eyes behind your counter.

But the shop isn't quite your shop anymore.

It floats in a warm soup color sky.

The hooks shine like stars.
Cash tickets are slowly falling from the ceiling.
The door bell rings on its own.

On the car, the same cat sleeps peacefully.

He doesn't seem surprised to see you.

He opens an eye, pushes your order book with his leg, then almost goes to sleep.

The notebook opens itself.

A sentence appears:

> When the world gets too cold, distributes something good.

Three doors appear in the shop.

The first beats to the rhythm of pressed clocks.
The second is closed by taped administrative paper.
The third flashes weakly, like a neon still extinct.

The cat jumps on the counter and looks at you.

Behind him, three doors. The shop floats in silence.

choices.text: Go through the clock door with your bag
Observe the cat of the dream

chat_hub.title: The Chat du Comptoir
chat_hub.narrator: Some guides prefer to sleep on the answers.
chat_hub.text:
The cat is lying on the car.

He opens an eye when you approach.

Around his neck hangs a small medal in the shape of a key.

You squat.

He puts a paw on the notebook, without rushing.

A new sentence appears:

> Bad habits are dreams that have forgotten to wake up.

The cat's yawning.

A check-in slips under his leg and falls on the counter.

On top, it says:

> Service included: smile, patience, human warmth.

choices.text: Ask him to show you the way

ville_intro.title: The City of Court
ville_intro.text:
You're coming to a town where everyone runs.

Passersby eat while walking.
The bikes go beyond the cars.
The clocks cry out the time instead of giving it.

Giant screens repeat:

**ALL VITE. **

**ALL TOO. **

**LOONED. **

Nobody's looking at each other.
Nobody really answers when we talk to him.

An old lady sits on a bench in the middle of the sidewalk.

A student crosses the street holding a pile of leaves that fly behind him.

A woman's talking on the phone.

**"Yes. Yes, yes. Yes, yes. *

She repeats it without ever listening to the answer.

In your hands, the blanquette smokes slowly.

It smells Sunday noon.

choices.text: Go talk to the old lady
you feel a delicious smell coming out of your bag, you can install a table and serve the blanquette

vieille_dame.title: The Lady of the Bench
vieille_dame.narrator: Sometimes someone waits less for an answer than a presence.
vieille_dame.text:
You sit next to her.

She doesn't seem surprised.

**"Everyone passes here, but nobody stays. *

She's looking at your bag.

**"It smells something I forgot. *

You serve him a plate.

She eats slowly.

Around you, three people slow down without understanding why.

A clock stops screaming.

The lady smiles.

**"You know, {player}, people think they're wasting time when they stop. But sometimes that's where they find him. *

The dream cat crosses the road slowly.

Very slowly.

The whole city has to slow down.

rue_sans_pause.title: Rue Sans Pause
rue_sans_pause.narrator: A city without a break ends up forgetting why it is moving forward.
rue_sans_pause.text:
You're watching passersby.

They're all running in the same direction.

But at the end of the avenue, there's nothing.

Only another avenue.

Then another one.

Then another one.

A mother holds her child's hand without looking at him.
A man swallows coffee without feeling his taste.
A student repeats: **"I'm almost late"**, over and over again.

The cat sits in the middle of the pedestrian crossing.

The horns become sighs.

You notice that everyone has something:
- a list,
- a phone,
- a schedule,
- a fear of being late.

But nobody's holding a plate.

choices.text: Install the large table in the middle of the avenue

etudiant_presse.title: Student Stressed
etudiant_presse.narrator: Success sometimes takes the form of a pile of leaves that flies away.
etudiant_presse.text:
The student picks up his leaves by shaking.

**"I'm late. *

A leaf flies away.

He repeats:

**"I'm late. *

You ask him where he should go.

He opens his mouth.

No answer comes out.

He looks at his leaves.

On each, the same sentence is written:

> BE LOST.

There's no date.

No address.

Just that injunction.

Your bag is between you two.

metro.title: Metro Sans Stop
metro.narrator: Some trains don't drive anywhere, but everyone gets there anyway.
metro.text:
The subway has been driving for a long time.

People look at the ground.

A man sleeps up.

One voice announced:

> Next stop:
> Later.

Nobody thinks it's strange.

A sign on the wall says:

> Save time while you lose your day.

The cat is lying on a reserved seat.

No one dares to ask him to move.

choices.text: Stay in the subway to see the next stop
Exit at the next imaginary stop

restaurant_express.title: Express Restaurant
restaurant_express.narrator: Here, the dishes arrive before hunger.
restaurant_express.text:
You're entering a tiny restaurant.

The waiter gives you the check before you even sit down.

On the tables, customers chew quickly.

Very quickly.

A dish comes in front of you.

You didn't order it.

The server says:

**"It's more efficient like that. *

In a corner, a woman looks at her empty plate.

She whispers:

**"I think I didn't feel the taste. *

choices.text: Open your bag and serve something real

table_ville.title: The Slowing Table
table_ville.narrator: The first act of resistance is sometimes to lay a tablecloth.
table_ville.text:
You set up a small table in the middle of the sidewalk.

That's absurd.

Then there's gotta be someone stopping.

Then two.

Then ten.

You're serving the blanquette on loose plates.

People eat fast first.

Then slow down.

Then they start talking.

A man takes his headphones off.
A woman finally puts her bag down.
The old lady laughs slowly.
The student finds a white sheet and decides not to write anything on it.

Clocks turn down.

The city doesn't stop.

She's breathing.

On the last giant screen, a new message appears:

> TAKE THE TIME TO GOT.

The old lady gets something out of her apron.

A bright snag. She's not saying anything. She just put it on your table.

**"You never know when a world will still need a break. *

choices.text: Move forward with the shiny ladle

fin_pas_le_temps.title: Not Time
fin_pas_le_temps.narrator: If we get there soon, we sometimes forget where we were going.
fin_pas_le_temps.text:
You start running.

At first, it's to follow the others.

Then not to bother.

Then because everyone runs.

Your steps are accelerating.

Clocks scream louder.

You pass the old lady on the bench.

She raises her hand.

You could stop.

But you don't have time.

The city is getting fuzzy.

One voice announced:

**"You will arrive soon. *

Then another adds:

**"But you don't know where. *

fin_retard.title: Delay
fin_retard.narrator: The delay is a loop with new shoes.
fin_retard.text:
You run with the student.

You turn left.

Then right.

Then left again.

The streets are repeated.

The screens are repeated.

Passersby repeat themselves.

After an impossible time to measure, you finally arrive.

In front of you:

the same avenue.

Same bench.

Same old lady.

The same student who picks up his leaves.

He's looking at you.

**"I'm late. *

And you understand that you are, too.

fin_metro.title: The Last Judgment
fin_metro.narrator: Some journeys are only long because you dare not go down.
fin_metro.text:
The subway continues.

The lights are flashing.

The voice announced:

> Next stop:
> Your retirement.

Nobody's moving.

The standing man always sleeps.

The cat goes down to a stop that doesn't exist.

The doors are closing.

You stay alone in the car.

The vote resumed:

> Next stop:
> You should have come down.

hub_admin.title: The Second Gate
hub_admin.narrator: Dreams don't let you go with one good action.
hub_admin.text:
You come back behind your floating counter.

The shiny ladle rests near the crate.

The clock door closed slowly.

The city that ran finally breathes.

But the other two doors stay here.

The morning door still doesn't appear.

The cat jumps on the counter, pushes the sausage from the end of the leg, then looks at the door covered with forms.

On the notebook, a new sentence reads:

> We can't wake up until the papers dream.

You understand.

The dream is not over.

choices.text: Pass the administrative door with your bag

admin_intro.title: The Forms Office
admin_intro.narrator: Here, the corridors are straight, but nobody gets to the end.
admin_intro.text:
You're arriving in a grey building.

Mountains of files go up to the ceiling.

Giant tampons hit papers on their own.

Behind the counters, faceless employees whisper:

**"One signature is missing. *

People everywhere are waiting with forms in their hands.

Some don't even remember what they were asking for.

Something in your bag smells good, even here.

A sign indicates:

> For any exit request, please request the exit request form.

Further on, you recognize the student from the city in a hurry.

This time, he has a file entitled:

> LOANING AUTHORISATION.

ticket_admin.title: Ticket A38
ticket_admin.narrator: Waiting becomes stranger when no one knows why.
ticket_admin.text:
You're taking a ticket.

It displays:

> A38

The light panel displays:

> A2

A woman has been waiting here for so long that she brought a plant.

The plant itself took a ticket.

You look at the student.

It has been filling the same line for several minutes.

Name:
Reason for Name:
Justification:

His hands are shaking.

The cat passes between the chairs and sleeps on the A3 ticket.

Nobody knows if the line can move on.

choices.text: Take something out of your bag and share it

ascenseur_admin.title: The Administrative Ascensor
ascenseur_admin.narrator: Even going up a floor sometimes requires a complete folder.
ascenseur_admin.text:
The elevator is at the end of the hall.

A metallic voice announces:

> Please provide proof of climb.

You press the button.

The vote resumed:

> Please provide proof that you have the right to support.

The cat sleeps in front of the buttons.

He's rubbing.

The elevator won't ask for anything anymore.

A faceless employee whispers:

**"It's been blocking the system since this morning. It's the quietest day we've had in years. *

choices.text: Enjoy the calm to organize a lunch break

trombones.title: The Trombone Man
trombones.narrator: Some tasks survive long after they have been forgotten.
trombones.text:
A man classifies trombones by color.

Red.
Blue.
Grey.
A little more administrative grey.

You ask him why.

He's thinking a long time.

Long time.

**"I don't know anymore. *

He's looking down.

**"But if I stop, someone might ask me what I really do here. *

You put something on his desk — something simple and real.

He's looking at him.

Then trombones.

Then the slice.

**"Is it... is it classed too? *

choices.text: Tell him some things are shared instead of classifying themselves

pause_dej.title: Bread, Saucisson and Tampons
pause_dej.narrator: An office becomes less terrible when someone breaks bread.
pause_dej.text:
You put the bread and the sausage on the counter.

A giant buffer stops in the middle of the flight.

A faceless employee turns his head.

**"This form is not intended. *

You cut a slice.

Then another one.

Someone laughs at the bottom of the line.

The files are shaking.

Papers fall from the ceiling like administrative confetti.

Gradually, employees find a face.

A woman remembers her request.
A man realizes he's been waiting at the wrong counter for three dreams.
The tampon clerk finds out he likes mustard.

The student closes his file.

On the cover, the title changes:

> AUTHORISATION TO RESPIRE.

Then he disappears.

Instead, there's a paper towel left.

And on the exit door, a sign appears:

> Thank you for coming back only if it is really necessary.

choices.text: Store an unnecessary buffer in your bag

admin_resolution.title: The Needless Stamp
admin_resolution.narrator: Sometimes the best tools are the ones you decide not to use anymore.
admin_resolution.text:
You get a little tampon on the counter.

It bears a strange mention:

> NOT IF GREAT.

You stamp the first file.

He disappears.

You're stamping an entire pile.

It becomes a pile of paper towels.

Employees get their breaks back.

The windows open.

The exit door lights up.

The dream cat sleeps on the most urgent issue.

No one dares to move him.

And, strangely enough, the world continues very well without this file.

fin_formulaire.title: The Final Form
fin_formulaire.narrator: Some traps start with a check box.
fin_formulaire.text:
You take the form.

He only has one page.

Then two.

Then twenty.

They ask you for your name, then the reason for your name, then a confirmation that you have a reason.

You sit down to fill more comfortably.

When you look up, you're behind a desk.

In front of you, someone's waiting.

You want to help him.

But a sentence comes out of your mouth before you can stop it:

**"One signature is missing. *

A tampon appears in your hand.

Again.

Again.

Again.

fin_employe.title: Monthly employee
fin_employe.narrator: Some promotions look like traps with an ergonomic chair.
fin_employe.text:
You wait.

The panel changes from A2 to A2 bis.

Then A2 additional.

Then A2 final version.

After a while, an employee gives you a badge.

> MONTH EMPLOYEE.

You're trying to explain that you're not employed here.

He's smiling at you.

**"That's what all the new people say. *

Your office appears.

Your chair appears.

Your installation form appears.

A note is placed on it:

> Your lunch break is scheduled in 17 years.

hub_market.title: The Third Gate
hub_market.narrator: One good thing sometimes calls for another.
hub_market.text:
You come back behind your floating counter.

The shiny ladle rests near the crate.
The useless buffer is now used as a clipboard.

Two doors closed.

The city is slowing down.
The administration is breathing.

But the neon of the third door still flashes.

The cat gets on the counter.

He sniffs the air, looks at you, then looks at the neon door.

On the notebook, a sentence appears:

> Some get lost running.
> Others get lost buying what they didn't need.

The morning door is still not there.

There's still a world to go through.

choices.text: Go through the neon door with your bag

market_intro.title: The Infinite Supermarket
market_intro.narrator: Here, everything is available. Except the exit.
market_intro.text:
You're coming to a huge supermarket.

The neons are buzzing.

The rays are repeated infinitely.

Promotion.
NEW.
FAMILY LOT.
LAST TIME.

Customers push full wagons without looking at what they take.

Nobody seems to be hungry.

Your bag's still here.

Something in it is still hot and smells good. Something goes through the rays like a little rebellion.

A voice in the speakers repeats:

**"Don't forget to buy what you didn't come for. *

You recognize the old lady in front of a canning radius.

You recognize the student in front of the prepared dishes.

You recognize the trombone man in front of a lot of 500 hangers.

They all look like they're looking for something.

Or forget what.

choices.text: Talking to the client with the overflowing cart
Install a table in the middle of the radius

cliente_chariot.title: The Cart too full
cliente_chariot.narrator: Some baskets are full of things we didn't really want.
cliente_chariot.text:
The client pushes a huge cart.

It overflows with identical boxes.

You ask her what she's looking for.

She's looking at her cart.

Then you.

**"I don't know anymore. There was a promotion. *

The cat sleeps in the child seat of the cart.

He seems perfectly satisfied.

You give him a plate of roast chicken.

She's breathing the smell.

His eyes are softening.

**"It reminds me of Sundays where we didn't hurry. *

It rests three boxes in the radius.

The cart becomes lighter.

So does she.

conserves.title: The Ray of the Conserves
conserves.narrator: When everything is preserved, even hesitations become long.
conserves.text:
The old lady reads the ingredients of a box of peas.

She's resting her.

She's taking another one.

She reads.

She's resting.

You ask her if she's looking for something.

**"I think I want to choose correctly. *

She looks at the infinite rays.

**"But the more choices, the less I know what I like. *

The student arrives with three identical prepared dishes.

**"This one looks more optimized. *

The cat drops a box.

She's driving to your feet.

On top of that, someone wrote:

> To share.

choices.text: Open the can and serve with the chicken
Compare all boxes one by one

caisses_auto.title: Automatic Caisses
caisses_auto.narrator: Some machines know very well how to say no.
caisses_auto.text:
The automatic body repeats:

> Unexpected item in the packing area.

You're watching.

There's nothing in the packing area.

The caisse takes over:

> Unexpected item in the packing area.

The cat jumps on the scanner.

BIP.

> Unexpected item in the packing area.

The cat is lying down.

BIP.

> Unexpected item in the packing area.

Behind you, a whole line awaits without protest.

A man whispers:

**"She's been doing this since Monday. *

choices.text: Unplug the box and invite the line to eat

table_market.title: The kitchen too big
table_market.narrator: When people remember they're hungry, the rays stop being infinite.
table_market.text:
You put the roast chicken on a small table.

Nobody knows where the table comes from.

But everyone understands what it's for.

The wagons are slowing down.

The ads are getting less strong.

A family sits down.
A student smiles shyly.
A man rests three objects he didn't need.
The old lady finally chooses a box, then decides she doesn't need it.

Soon, ray 12 doesn't look like a trap.

It looks like a kitchen too big.

Promotions become clawed revenue.

An ad goes into the speakers:

**"The store will close when everyone finds out what he had come for. *

The client with the overflowing cart gives you a little neon light.

**"For places too cold. *

choices.text: Moving forward with the little neon light

fin_carte.title: Loyalty Card
fin_carte.narrator: Some rewards cost more than purchases.
fin_carte.text:
You follow promotions.

A loyalty card appears in your hand.

Then a second.

Then a third.

Each card promises more benefits than the previous one.

One voice announced:

> Congratulations, you are now a priority customer.

The rays are opening in front of you.

Your cart is growing.

Your pockets fill with tickets.

You don't know what you wanted to buy anymore.

But you won 3% on something.

Later, much later, you sleep in the bedding department.

The car wishes you a good night.

fin_bug.title: Article Unexpected
fin_bug.narrator: Maybe the bug was the only thing that was still sincere.
fin_bug.text:
You keep scanning.

BIP.

> Unexpected item in the packing area.

BIP.

> Unexpected item in the packing area.

BIP.

> Unexpected item in the packing area.

The world is shaking.

Neon flashes.

Customers freeze.

The cat is watching you.

He seems almost disappointed.

The caisse takes over:

> Unexpected customer in the existence zone.

Then the whole world freeze.

fin_rayon_12.title: Section 12
fin_rayon_12.narrator: All outings look like promotions when you don't know what you're looking for.
fin_rayon_12.text:
You compare the boxes.

Extra fine peas.
Fine peas.
Almost sincere.
Limited edition peas.
Peas with promise of happiness.

The rays are repeated.

Your basket is getting heavier.

You don't remember taking anything.

There's a jingle in the speakers.

**"Exceptional offer. Exceptional offer. Exceptional offer. *

You're passing the 12th.

Then again.

Then again.

After a while, you understand.

You're not looking for the exit anymore.

You're only looking for the next ray.

final_hub.title: The Calmed Counter
final_hub.narrator: The dream keeps track of what we warmed up.
final_hub.text:
You come back behind your floating counter.

This time, everything is different.

The shiny ladle rests near the crate.
The useless stamp serves as a clipboard.
The little neon light gently illuminates the notebook.

All three doors are closed.

Behind them, the worlds don't scream anymore.

The city in a hurry is breathing.
The administration has regained its break.
The infinite supermarket almost looks like a kitchen too big.

The cat jumps on the counter.

He walks between the three objects, as if he were checking that everything was finally in his place.

Then he sits in front of a new door.

A simple door.

A shop door.

The notebook opens on the last page.

> You didn't feed clients.
> You reminded people that they existed together.

The door leads to the morning.

This time you can wake up.

But behind you, a huge table begins to appear.

banquet.title: The Banquet of Ordinary Worlds
banquet.location: REVE — A BIG TABLE BETWEEN THE WORLDS
banquet.narrator: The colder worlds sometimes repair themselves around a table too large.
banquet.text:
You're serving people in a hurry.

You cut the sausage for the workers in the forms.

You share the roast chicken with the customers of the infinite supermarket.

Nobody knows exactly where he is.

But everyone knows what to do.

Sit down.

Eat.

Talk.

Laugh a little.

Clocks put their hands on the table.
Forms become tablecloths.
Promotion tickets change into recipes.

The student talks with the trombone man.
The old lady laughs with the client of the cart.
The woman on the phone hung up.

The cat jumps on the big table.

He steals a little piece of roasted chicken.

Nobody's hunting him.

For once, no one is in a hurry.

You look at everyone.

Then you understand that you have not overcome bad habits.

You reminded them they didn't have to lead life.

When you finally open the morning door, the real shop is waiting for you.

And this time, you know exactly why you're opening it.

reveil.title: Morning Opens
reveil.narrator: Some doors open before you even touch the handle.
reveil.text:
You're slowly opening your eyes.

The morning light crosses the shop window.

For a few seconds...

Don't move.

You hear:

- a truck in the distance,
- birds,
- the old fridge that's stuffing gently.

Then something jumps on the counter.

The cat.

He's looking at you.

Very calmly.

Then he puts a leg on your order book.

Like in the dream.

You stay silent.

The cat mials once.

Then he settles in a ball near the car.

Someone knocks on the door.

You turn the sign over.

> OPEN

The bell rings.

A client comes in.

Hello, {player}}. As usual? *

You're smiling.

And for the first time in a long time...

The shop looks really alive.
